'''Wayne McDonald''' (born {{birth date|1975|9|3|df=y}}) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. McDonald appeared in the [[Super League]] playing over 175 games in the 1990s and 2000s. McDonald played his club football for the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], [[Hull FC]], [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], [[Leeds Rhinos]], [[Wigan Warriors]] and the [[Huddersfield Giants]]. McDonald retired at the end of the 2006 Super League season ending his 12-year-long career. McDonald was also the tallest player to have played in Super League standing at {{convert|6|ft|7|in|m|abbr=on}}, until surpassed by [[Corentin Le Cam]] in 2021 who stands at {{convert|6|ft|9|in|m|abbr=on}}.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.thefreelibrary.com/Dragons+face+Giants+fire.(Sport)-a0146832378|title = Dragons face Giants fire. - Free Online Library}}</ref>

McDonald represented [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] between 1999 and 2005, featuring in the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]], and captaining the team in the European Tri-nations tournaments.{{citation needed|date=March 2020}}

McDonald played for the Huddersfield Giants in the [[2006 Challenge Cup]] Final from the interchange bench against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] but the Huddersfield Giants lost 12–42.{{citation needed|date=March 2020}}

McDonald also played for the {{rlnt|United Arab Emirates}} in 2009.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.lasttackle.com/news_2250-former-sl-man-stars-for-emirates-rl.html |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110713234647/http://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_2250-former-sl-man-stars-for-emirates-rl.html |access-date=24 June 2010 |archive-date=13 July 2011 |title=Former SL man stars for Emirates RL | Love Rugby League }}</ref>
